Reporting Arbitrated Results

After a game ends, each client individually calls the XOnlineArbitrationReport function to send the complete game report to the arbitration service. The submitted stats info should include stat changes to all players participating in the session and must match exactly for all clients that still have connectivity at the end of the game. Before sending a score report, make sure that any stats or competition results submitted by each Xbox at the end of the game session match exactly, byte for byte.

The arbitration service collects and validates the client reports and either invalidates the session or records the final results. The service then updates the player reliability ratings for all involved parties.

Note that when your title submits the results with XOnlineCompetitionSubmitResults, it can perform a quick check to help prevent cheating. Two of the XOnlineCompetitionSubmitResults function's parameters, pStatProcs and pAttributes, point to arrays of structures. These structures must identically match the arrays from every other player when compared at the binary level. Mismatched results are a signal to the arbitration service that one or more clients might be using an altered version of your title to submit bogus scores. This results in problematic sessions that may affect competition results. During development, titles should use debug code to compare and match submitted results and eliminate all cases where non-matching results are sent to the arbitration service for sessions that should have completed correctly.

Competitions are handled exactly like regular games with these exceptions:

  • The final results are posted both in stats and in the competitions service.
  • The updates to player reliability ratings are amplified depending on which tournament flags are set.
